outbound_official.notes: Official MFA page titled 'Visa requirements for citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan' (O'zbekiston Respublikasi fuqarolari uchun viza talablari). Page last updated 2025-12-29. Kenya, Côte d'Ivoire, Seychelles, and Sri Lanka listed under visa-free but may require tourist voucher/hotel confirmation and return ticket. Jordan added to visa-free per Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Qatar and Saudi Arabia appear as visa-on-arrival on this page (consistent with the December 2025 snapshot). Page is in Russian/Uzbek; no equivalent English-language page was retrievable from gov.uz.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (T)
visa_types.purpose: Issued for tourism and leisure travel to Uzbekistan. Covers sightseeing, cultural visits, and recreation.
visa_types.notes: Standard single-entry tourist visa issued via Uzbekistan embassy or consulate. Fee is for up to 30-day stay. Longer validity options available at higher fees: 7 days $40, 15 days $50, 30 days $60, 3 months $80, 6 months $120, 1 year $160.

visa_types.name: Group Tourist Visa (TG)
visa_types.purpose: Tourist entry visa for organized groups of 5 or more foreign nationals visiting Uzbekistan for tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Reduced per-person fee of $15–$25 for groups of 5 or more persons. Must be organized through a licensed Uzbekistan tour operator.

visa_types.name: Electronic Visa - Single Entry (e-visa)
visa_types.purpose: Online tourist/visit visa for eligible nationalities allowing a single entry stay in Uzbekistan for up to 30 days.
visa_types.notes: Available for 76+ eligible countries via e-visa.gov.uz. E-visa is valid for 90 days from date of issue; single entry for up to 30 days stay. Available for tourist, business, and other purposes under one application type.

visa_types.name: Electronic Visa - Double Entry (e-visa)
visa_types.purpose: Online visa allowing two entries to Uzbekistan, each for up to 30 days, suitable for multi-leg itineraries.
visa_types.notes: Double-entry e-visa for 30-day stays per entry. Valid for 90 days from issuance.

visa_types.name: Electronic Visa - Multiple Entry (e-visa)
visa_types.purpose: Online multiple-entry visa for eligible nationalities, allowing repeated entries into Uzbekistan within the validity period.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry e-visa; each stay up to 30 days. Valid for 90 days from issuance.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (B-2)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Uzbekistan for general business activities including meetings, negotiations, and commercial visits.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year. Requires invitation from a registered Uzbekistan legal entity. Multiple-entry fee for 1 year is $250.

visa_types.name: Accredited Business Representative Visa (B-1)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who are officially accredited as representatives of foreign banks, commercial offices, or trade companies in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the accreditation period. Requires formal accreditation with Uzbekistan authorities.

visa_types.name: Work Visa (Is)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals authorized to work in Uzbekistan under a legal employment contract or labor agreement.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior approval from the Agency for External Labor Migration. Employer must obtain a work permit before visa is issued.

visa_types.name: Student Visa (A-1)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals enrolled full-time in an educational institution in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year, renewable. Requires acceptance letter and enrollment confirmation from an accredited Uzbekistan educational institution.

visa_types.name: Short-Term Student Exchange Visa (STD)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals participating in short-term student exchange programs with Uzbekistan educational institutions.
visa_types.notes: For structured exchange programs. Validity aligned with program duration, up to 1 year.

visa_types.name: Teaching / Educational Staff Visa (A-2)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals taking up teaching positions at educational institutions in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Requires contract with an accredited Uzbekistan educational institution. Valid up to 1 year.

visa_types.name: Academic Research / Lecturer Visa (A-3)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign scientists, researchers, and lecturers conducting academic research or delivering lectures at Uzbekistan institutions.
visa_types.notes: Validity ranges from 3 months to 2 years depending on the research or lecture program duration.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (TRAN)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals transiting through Uzbekistan territory en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Allows transit passage of up to 3 days. Available at embassy/consulate and also at Tashkent International Airport for same-day transit. Uzbekistan Airways transit passengers may qualify for a 5-day visa-free transit procedure.

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment Visa (WITH)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Uzbekistan to receive medical treatment at a licensed healthcare facility.
visa_types.notes: Requires confirmation letter from an accredited medical institution in Uzbekistan. Valid up to 3 months.

visa_types.name: Private / Guest Visit Visa - Invited by Uzbek Citizen (PV-1)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals visiting Uzbekistan at the invitation of a citizen or permanent resident of Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Inviting party must be a citizen or permanent resident of Uzbekistan. Valid up to 1 year.

visa_types.name: Private / Guest Visit Visa - Invited by Foreign Resident (PV-2)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals visiting Uzbekistan at the invitation of a foreign national legally residing in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Inviting party must be a foreign national holding a valid residence permit in Uzbekistan. Valid up to 1 year.

visa_types.name: Compatriot Family Visit Visa (VTD)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who are relatives of Uzbek citizens residing abroad, visiting family members in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 2 years. Intended for relatives of Uzbek diaspora members or compatriots. Documentation of family relationship required.

visa_types.name: Investment Visa (INV)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry visa issued to foreign investors who have made qualifying investments in Uzbekistan, facilitating repeated business travel related to their investment.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 3 years, multiple entry. Minimum investment threshold is approximately USD 8,500 or more to qualify. Different from the RBI golden visa/residence permit programs which require USD 100,000–$3,000,000+.

visa_types.name: Pilgrimage / Religious-Cultural Heritage Visa (PLG)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals visiting Uzbekistan for religious pilgrimages or study of cultural and religious heritage sites.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 2 months. Intended for religious visitors to Uzbekistan's historic Islamic sites (Samarkand, Bukhara, etc.) and cultural heritage travel.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Entry Visa - Permanent Accreditation (D-1)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign diplomatic staff with permanent accreditation to Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the duration of the accreditation period. Issued to holders of diplomatic passports with full accreditation to Uzbekistan. No fee applies to diplomatic visas.

visa_types.name: Temporary Diplomatic Stay Visa (D-2)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary stay visa for diplomatic passport holders on short-term missions or visits to Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 3 months. For diplomatic passport holders on temporary assignment.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Tourist Visa (DT)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to diplomatic passport holders travelling to Uzbekistan for personal tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Up to 1 month. For private tourism by holders of diplomatic passports.

visa_types.name: Service Entry Visa - Permanent Accreditation (S-1)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for embassy support staff, NGO representatives, and service-category personnel with permanent accreditation.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the accreditation period. For service passport holders with formal accreditation.

visa_types.name: Temporary Service Stay Visa (S-2)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary stay visa for service passport holders on short-term official missions in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 3 months.

visa_types.name: Service Entry Visa - State Organization Invitation (S-3)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals invited by Uzbekistan state organizations for official business or project-related visits.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year. Requires formal invitation from a recognized Uzbekistan state body.

visa_types.name: Official / State Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to members of official state delegations visiting Uzbekistan for government-level meetings and state visits.
visa_types.notes: Validity aligned with the duration of the official event. Restricted to official government delegations.

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa - Permanent Accreditation (J-1)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign journalists and media representatives with permanent accreditation to work in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the accreditation period. Requires accreditation from the Uzbekistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs press service.

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa - Temporary Accreditation (J-2)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign journalists on temporary assignment or event coverage in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the duration of the temporary accreditation or assignment.

visa_types.name: Crew Member Visa (C-1)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to crew members of international aircraft and railway services transiting or operating in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year, multiple entry. For international transport crew members on commercial routes.

visa_types.name: Freight Driver Visa (C-2)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign commercial freight vehicle drivers operating on international routes through or into Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year, multiple entry. For international truck drivers on commercial freight routes.

visa_types.name: Emergency Exit Visa (EXIT)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who have overstayed their permitted period and require authorization to depart Uzbekistan.
visa_types.notes: Up to 1 month. Issued to resolve overstay situations and allow lawful departure. Applied for at the relevant immigration authority.

visa_types.name: Group Electronic Visa (e-visa group)
visa_types.purpose: Online group visa application for organized tourist groups of 5 or more persons from eligible countries.
visa_types.notes: Per-person fee of $20+ for group e-visa applications. Group must have a minimum of 5 persons from eligible e-visa nationalities. Applied via e-visa.gov.uz portal.

visa_free.notes: Upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025 under the Uzbekistan-China mutual visa-free agreement signed December 1, 2024 in Chengdu (confirmed by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025). Maximum 90 days within any 180-day period. IMPORTANT: Does NOT apply to visits to Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR - those require a separate visa. Does not cover work, study, or media activities.
30-day visa-free; confirmed on official Uzbekistan MFA visa page.

Reverted to 10-day visa-free per official MFA visa page, which lists Bahrain under 'Up to 10 days' (entry 87) and states: 'Citizens of Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman and China are granted a visa-free regime for a period of 10 days, provided they have a flight ticket to their home country or a third country.' The 30-day claim was sourced only to the kursiv.media aggregator and is contradicted by the official source; reverted on 2026-06-27 re-verification.

Reverted to 10-day visa-free per official MFA visa page, which lists Kuwait under 'Up to 10 days' (entry 88) and states: 'Citizens of Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman and China are granted a visa-free regime for a period of 10 days, provided they have a flight ticket to their home country or a third country.' The 30-day claim was sourced only to the kursiv.media aggregator and is contradicted by the official source; reverted on 2026-06-27 re-verification.

Reverted to 10-day visa-free per official MFA visa page, which lists Oman under 'Up to 10 days' (entry 89) and states: 'Citizens of Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman and China are granted a visa-free regime for a period of 10 days, provided they have a flight ticket to their home country or a third country.' The 30-day claim was sourced only to the kursiv.media aggregator and is contradicted by the official source; reverted on 2026-06-27 re-verification.
Was 10-day visa-free (missing from prior record). Extended to 30-day visa-free under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
Added to 30-day visa-free regime under Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Previously eligible only for e-visa. Saudi Arabia is also eligible for e-visa via e-visa.gov.uz.
Bilateral visa-free agreement signed during Uzbekistan-Jordan summit in Samarkand (August 2025); entered into force September 25, 2025 for 30 days. Previously required a visa.
Visa-free effective January 1, 2026, per Presidential Decree No. 203 of November 3, 2025. Now in force.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival available at Tashkent International Airport for citizens of countries without an Uzbekistan diplomatic mission in their country, requiring prior confirmation from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs

e_visa.notes: Single entry $20, double entry $35, multiple entry $50; e-visa valid 90 days from issuance
Also eligible for visa-free entry since June 2025
Includes Hong Kong SAR; note that Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR are excluded from the June 2025 mutual visa-free agreement and may still require e-visa
Listed as Macedonia in original UN source
Also eligible for visa-free entry since June 2025
Also eligible for visa-free entry since October 2025
Also eligible for visa-free entry since January 1, 2026

visa_policy.notes: CIS member states (Azerbaijan, Georgia, Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Moldova, Russia, Ukraine) have unlimited visa-free stay; Kyrgyzstan 60 days; Tajikistan 30 days. Most of Europe, Americas, Asia-Pacific and select Middle East/Africa/Caribbean states: 30 days. China upgraded to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025 (mutual agreement) - excludes HK/Macao SAR. Mauritius: 30-day visa-free confirmed on official MFA page. Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman: 10-day visa-free per official MFA page (October 2025 30-day upgrade claimed in secondary sources only). Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan: 30-day visa-free per Cabinet of Ministers resolution No. 7782935 of October 21, 2025 (secondary source; MFA activity page may not yet reflect this). UAE: 30-day visa-free established March 20, 2019. USA visa-free from January 1, 2026 (Presidential Decree No. 203, Nov 3, 2025) - now in force. E-visa (e-visa.gov.uz) available for 76+ countries at USD 20 single entry. Transit visa-free (5 days) for transit passengers on Uzbekistan Airways.

conditional_access.conditions: Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of China (up to 30 days). Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.
Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Hungary (up to 30 days). Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Turkmenistan (up to 30 days). Ordinary passport holders of Turkmenistan require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Vietnam (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders of Vietnam require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of India (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders of India require an e-visa. Note: accredited diplomats only (except India, which is excluded from the unlimited-period accredited-diplomat provision).

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of the Republic of Korea (up to 60 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 60-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Brazil (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Egypt (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders of Egypt require a visa. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Kuwait (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders of Kuwait are visa-free for 10 days per the official MFA page (the October 2025 30-day upgrade is recorded in visa_policy with source_official:false); diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Latvia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of the UAE (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Poland (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Romania (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Singapore (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Slovakia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Note: some aggregator sources indicate this covers 'diplomatic and service passports' but official gov.uz page specifies 'diplomatic passports'. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Turkey (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Estonia (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

Visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports of Japan (up to 90 days). Ordinary passport holders are visa-free for 30 days; diplomatic passport holders receive an extended 90-day waiver. Accredited diplomatic mission employees and family members may stay for the entire period of work without a visa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists. Discussions about a potential $1 million investment CBI program have occurred but as of 2026 no official program has been launched. Uzbekistan generally does not allow dual citizenship.

programs.name: Golden Visa - Donation Route
programs.notes: 5-year residence permit via one-off donation of USD 250,000 for main applicant; USD 150,000 per additional family member (spouse, children, parents). No nationality restrictions. No requirement to form a business or purchase real estate. Introduced by Presidential Decree No. UP-67 of April 18, 2025; effective June 1, 2025. Path to citizenship not confirmed officially.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Business Investment - Standard
programs.notes: 3-year renewable residence permit for investment of USD 250,000 in a company registered in Uzbekistan. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Business Investment - Large Scale
programs.notes: 10-year renewable residence permit for investment of USD 3,000,000 in a business engaged in production of goods or services. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate - Tashkent/Tashkent Region
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 300,000 in Tashkent city or Tashkent region. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate - Major Cities
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 200,000 in cities of Samarkand, Bukhara, Namangan, Andijan, Fergana, or Khorezm. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Residence Permit via Real Estate - Other Regions
programs.notes: Residence permit via real estate investment of USD 100,000 in other regions including Republic of Karakalpakstan. Based on amendments to Presidential Decree No. UP-5611.

programs.name: Investment Visa (3-year)
programs.eligibility: Foreign investors who have made an investment of USD 3 million or more in Uzbekistan
programs.notes: 3-year investor visa for foreign investors. Confirmed on Embassy of Uzbekistan in the United States official site.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: China (CHN) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free. Mutual Uzbekistan-China visa-free agreement signed December 1, 2024 in Chengdu, entered into force June 1, 2025 (source: gov.uz/en/mfa/news/view/53659). Confirmed by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025. Maximum stay 30 days per entry, 90 days within any 180-day period. Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR are explicitly EXCLUDED from this agreement.
CORRECTION: Bahrain (BHR) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Prior record had incorrect note 'proposed but not yet confirmed as enacted' - the extension is now in force.
CORRECTION: Kuwait (KWT) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Same as Bahrain - prior record incorrectly marked as 'proposed'.
CORRECTION: Oman (OMN) upgraded from 10-day to 30-day visa-free effective June 1, 2025. Same correction as Bahrain and Kuwait.

ADDITION: Qatar (QAT) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Was previously listed at 10-day visa-free in official sources (alongside Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, China) but was MISSING from the prior record entirely. Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 extended Qatar to 30 days.

ADDITION: Saudi Arabia (SAU) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Was previously only in e_visa list. Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 granted Saudi Arabia ordinary-passport visa-free access for 30 days.
ADDITION: Jordan (JOR) added to visa_free list at 30 days. Completely missing from prior record. Added by Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025.
CONFIRMED: USA (USA) visa-free from January 1, 2026 per Presidential Decree No. 203 (Nov 3, 2025) - now in force as of validation date (June 2026). Notes updated to remove future-tense language.
CONFIRMED: Tajikistan (TJK) is correctly 30 days (not 60 days) - confirmed by current official source.
NOTE: The China e_visa entry note updated to clarify that Hong Kong SAR holders may still require e-visa as HK/Macao SAR are excluded from the mutual visa-free agreement.
ADDITION: visa_types field populated with 32 visa categories extracted from official MFA page gov.uz/en/mfa/activity_page/o-zbekiston-respublikasi-vizasi and e-visa.gov.uz portal.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched official MFA page (gov.uz/en/mfa/activity_page/o-zbekiston-respublikasi-vizasi). Key finding: MFA page still shows Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman at 10 days and does not reflect October 21, 2025 Cabinet resolution upgrades to 30 days. Source for Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman 30-day entries corrected from source_official:true (gov.uz MFA page) to source_official:false (kursiv.media secondary source confirming October 21 Cabinet resolution). Jordan source updated to bilateral agreement effective September 25, 2025 (gazeta.uz). This is consistent: the MFA page appears not to have been updated to reflect the October 2025 resolution.

2026-06-27 REMOVAL: Mauritius (MUS) removed from visa_policy.visa_free. The official MFA inbound visa page does NOT list Mauritius as a visa-free country. Mauritius appears only in the OUTBOUND section (Uzbek citizens get visa-on-arrival to Mauritius) and as a separate unrelated entry. Keeping Mauritius as visa-free would be a false visa-free error. Moved to gaps for future verification.

2026-06-27 ADDITION: Mongolia (MNG) added to visa_policy.visa_free at 30 days. Mongolia was in the outbound visa_free list and confirmed by the official MFA page as a 30-day visa-free country for inbound travel to Uzbekistan, but was missing from the inbound visa_policy.visa_free list.

2026-06-27 JORDAN SOURCE CORRECTION: Jordan entry source updated to bilateral agreement source (gazeta.uz/en/2025/08/29/jordan/); effective date confirmed as September 25, 2025 via bilateral agreement. Prior note only cited October 21 Cabinet resolution.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched official MFA page (gov.uz/en/mfa/activity_page/o-zbekiston-respublikasi-vizasi). Key findings: (1) Mauritius IS listed as 30-day visa-free on the official MFA page. The 2026-06-27 removal was incorrect. Mauritius ADDED BACK to visa_policy.visa_free at 30 days with source_official:true. (2) China, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar continue to appear in the '10 days' group on the MFA activity page — but the specific bilateral agreement page (gov.uz/en/mfa/news/view/53659) confirmed China is 30 days effective June 1, 2025. Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman remain at 10 days in the record (source_official:true from MFA page). The October 2025 Cabinet resolution for Qatar/Saudi Arabia/Jordan 30-day entries remains at source_official:false. (3) UAE confirmed as 30-day visa-free from March 2019 (dubai.mfa.uz). (4) USA confirmed as 30-day visa-free from January 1, 2026 (gov.uz/en/eco/news/view/99182). (5) Kuwait conditional_access note corrected to reflect that the official MFA page shows 10 days for ordinary passports, not 30 days. Sources reached this run: gov.uz MFA activity page, gov.uz/en/mfa/news/view/53659, gov.uz/en/eco/news/view/99182, dubai.mfa.uz, uz.kursiv.media, web searches.
gaps: The complete official enumeration of all e-visa eligible countries (full Appendix 1 referenced in gov.uz decree) could not be directly accessed as e-visa.gov.uz renders dynamically; the list has reportedly expanded to 76+ countries as of 2025.
Max stay days for visa-on-arrival not confirmed from official source - only described as available with prior MFA confirmation.
Path to permanent residency and citizenship from RBI programs not confirmed from official source.

Whether Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR holders require e-visa or standard visa was not confirmed from a directly-fetched official source; the China mutual agreement explicitly excludes them, suggesting they still need e-visa or regular visa.

The Cabinet of Ministers resolution of October 21, 2025 source was confirmed only via secondary news sources (kursiv.media, zamin.uz - not *.gov.uz domains); a direct gov.uz URL for this resolution was not obtained. Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Oman 30-day entries are now all marked source_official: false as a result. The MFA activity page still shows Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman at 10 days as of June 2026.
The 'My Second Home' 1-year tourist visa program - while announced - could not be confirmed with a direct official gov.uz URL.
Dual citizenship policy for RBI/golden visa holders not confirmed from official source.
CIS free movement: Uzbekistan is in CIS but not EAEU; the unlimited stay for certain CIS nationals is based on bilateral agreements not a formal CIS-wide freedom of movement treaty.
The updated 76-country e-visa list was not individually enumerated from an official source.

No official Uzbekistan government source found confirming any conditional entry rule based on holding a foreign visa (US, Schengen, UK, etc.) - credential-based conditional access appears absent from Uzbekistan's policy.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or equivalent special diaspora/heritage status exemption found from any official Uzbekistan government source.

For Latvia, Romania, and Slovakia: some aggregator sources state 'diplomatic and service passports' are covered, but the official gov.uz page consistently states 'diplomatic passports' only. The service passport scope for these three countries could not be verified from a directly-fetched official source.

Bilateral visa-exemption agreements for diplomatic and service passports signed with Albania (September 2024) and others may be pending ratification and have not been recorded as in-force conditional access rules.
Exact fee amounts for most non-tourist/business visa categories (student, work, medical, family, etc.) not specified on official MFA page.
Mauritius (MUS): RESOLVED in 2026-06-29 re-verification. Mauritius is confirmed as 30-day visa-free on the official MFA page and has been added back to visa_policy.visa_free.

Saudi Arabia's initial visa-free start date: the October 21, 2025 Cabinet resolution is the confirmed source for Saudi Arabia's inclusion; no bilateral treaty effective date was separately found. Effective date assumed October 21, 2025 or shortly after.
